era_context: The Han Dynasty is in advanced decline following the Yellow Turban Rebellion (184–205 CE). Regional warlords are consolidating power. The Roman Empire under Commodus continues to experience political instability. The Kushan Empire dominates Central Asia and the Indian subcontinent.

events.title: Yellow Turban Rebellion: Continued suppression and warlord consolidation
events.description: The Yellow Turban Rebellion, which began in 184 CE, continued into 186 CE with ongoing military campaigns by Han forces and regional warlords. By 186 CE, the rebellion had been largely suppressed in most regions, but the process of suppression had empowered regional military commanders who would soon challenge Han central authority. The warlord period was beginning to take shape.

sources.name: Records of the Three Kingdoms (Sanguozhi) by Chen Shou

events.certainty_note: The Yellow Turban Rebellion is well-attested, but specific events in 186 CE are poorly documented. The date is assigned by convention.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 186 CE is sparse. Chinese sources focus on the Yellow Turban Rebellion, but specific events in 186 CE are rarely mentioned by name. The year falls within the broader period of the rebellion's suppression and the consolidation of warlord power, but no specific events for 186 CE are clearly documented in surviving sources.

graph_edges.note: Continuation of the Yellow Turban Rebellion
